channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Infamous Clémenceau metro shooters in Brussels jailed for over 10 years
item.description: Images of the young man holding and firing an AK-47 inside a Brussels Metro station were circulated across the world.

item.title: Investigation into damage at Cinquantenaire Park following US July 4th party
item.description: The celebration, marking the 250th anniversary of the United States, took place under the triumphal arch of the Cinquantenaire Park in Brussels.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 3 - 5 July
item.description: From one of Europe's biggest music festivals to centuries-old traditions in Grand Place, there are plenty of ways to make the most of the first weekend of July.

item.title: Could Brussels become a '15-minute city’ and how might it benefit residents?
item.description: The concept has been hailed as a framework to improve quality of life, public health and sustainability, but the capital's transport nightmares and uneven resources risk keeping the dream out of reach.

item.title: The Brussels economy minister has a strategy for jobs, growth and Midi – but will it change anything?
item.description: "We are not New York. We are not Manchester. We are Brussels. We shouldn't be willing to be something else than who we are," Laurent Hublet told The Brussels Times.

item.title: Plan to replace Metro 3 project with tram lines sent to Brussels Region
item.description: The proposed plan, called Prémétro+, suggests rerouting tram lines 51 and 82 to run above ground between Midi station and Lemonnier.

item.title: Ommegang transports Brussels back to Renaissance
item.description: Ommegang is one of Brussels' best-loved spectacles, featuring jousting, crossbows, and a procession of around 1,400 participants.

item.title: Original bronze shields stolen from iconic Brussels fountain
item.description: The City of Brussels had thoroughly renovated the monument in 2019, with the recent damage said to be worth tens of thousands of euros.

item.title: Brussels Metro lines 1 and 5 interrupted this summer
item.description: The line will be partially interrupted to test out a new signalling technology, which forms part of a wider modernisation of the metro network.

item.title: Children get sick after playing in Brussels fountain during heatwave
item.description: One of the children, 4, developed a high fever and rash after spending an afternoon running through the fountains.

item.title: Late-night joy in Brussels for Belgium's dramatic World Cup win (photos)
item.description: Celebrations kicked off just before 01:00 in Brussels and around the country after Belgium eliminated Senegal in the last few minutes of extra-time.

item.title: Historic bridge in Brussels’ Maritime District reopens to the public
item.description: Near Tour & Taxis, the bridge lies at a junction of various transport links for the people of Brussels.

item.title: Man injured in stabbing in Brussels
item.description: The man was stabbed in the abdominal area with a sharp object and had to be taken to hospital for treatment.

item.title: 'Loud minority': Most Brussels residents support Low Emission Zone, survey shows
item.description: "There is a difference between what the majority of the population thinks and what a small minority of the population shouts about very loudly."

item.title: Multilingualism in Belgium: What happens in our brains when we switch languages?
item.description: For residents of Brussels, switching between languages is a part of everyday life – but it comes with a heavy cognitive workload.

item.title: City of Brussels to make live-out landlords pay more property tax
item.description: In the City of Brussels, only 30% of properties are owner-occupied.

item.title: Police urged to act amid alarming increase in e-scooter deaths and injuries in Belgium
item.description: Brussels is banning shared e-scooters from 2027 and new data on road traffic accidents helps to explain why.

item.title: Summer transport chaos in Saint-Gilles as construction work disrupts buses and trams
item.description: “Passengers in Saint-Gilles have had enough,” said the Brussels Public Transport Users’ Association.

item.title: Wallonia led Belgium's new business growth last year - here's why
item.description: Wallonia recorded a 12.1% growth in new firms in 2025, with Hainaut recording an increase of almost 20%.

item.title: Letters from Europe's darkest chapter could soon be etched on a new Brussels memorial
item.description: If plans come together as expected, Brussels will host a major new place of remembrance.
